searchDocs.title: byName​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.byName( matchName: string-- The string pattern to match against the module's name. ) → Predicate Creates a predicate function that matches a module's name against a string.

searchDocs.title: loadChildren​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Bootstrapper.loadChildren( parent: Instance ,-- The Instance to search within. predicate:: Predicate?-- Optional filter to include specific modules (e.g., Bootstrapper.byName("Service$")). ) → LoadedModules,Errors? Requires all direct child ModuleScripts within a given parent. Returns an array of loaded modules to guarantee order execution.

searchDocs.title: loadDescendants​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Bootstrapper.loadDescendants( parent: Instance ,-- The Instance to search within. predicate:: Predicate?-- Optional filter to include specific modules (e.g., Bootstrapper.byName("Service$")). ) → LoadedModules,Errors? Requires all descendant ModuleScripts within a given parent. Returns an array of loaded modules to guarantee order execution.

searchDocs.title: loadSequence​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Bootstrapper.loadSequence( modules: ModuleScripts,-- Array of ModuleScripts. predicate:: Predicate?-- Optional filter to include specific modules (e.g., Bootstrapper.byName("Service$")). ) → LoadedModules,Errors? Requires specific modules in a guaranteed sequence array.

searchDocs.title: run​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Bootstrapper.run( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts. methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ...: any-- Additional arguments to pass to the method. ) → LoadedModules,Errors? Executes methods one-by-one and yields the caller until complete. Guarantees that Module A is finished before Module B starts.

searchDocs.title: runAsync​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.runAsync( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts. methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ...: any-- Additional arguments to pass to the method. ) → () Executes methods one-by-one in a new thread. Maintains order but does not block the calling script.

searchDocs.title: runParallel​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.runParallel( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts. methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ...: any-- Additional arguments to pass to the method. ) → () Executes all methods simultaneously using multiple threads. Fastest execution, but order of completion is not guaranteed.

searchDocs.title: bindTo​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.bindTo( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts. methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. event: Event-- An RBXScriptSignal, custom Signal or a subscription function. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Binds a method across all modules to an RBXScriptSignal, a custom Signal, or a subscription function.

searchDocs.title: bindToHeartbeat​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.Heartbeat.

searchDocs.title: bindToRenderStep​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bootstrapper.bindToRenderStep( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. priority: number?-- Optional execution priority. If provided, uses BindToRenderStep instead of RenderStepped. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.RenderStepped or RunService:BindToRenderStep if a priority is given.

searchDocs.title: bindToPreSimulation​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.bindToPreSimulation( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string,-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. frequency: Enum.StepFrequency?-- Optional execution frequency. If provided, uses BindToSimulation instead of PreSimulation. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.PreSimulation or RunService:BindToSimulation if a frequency is given.

searchDocs.title: bindToPostSimulation​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.bindToPostSimulation( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.PostSimulation.

searchDocs.title: bindToPreAnimation​
searchDocs.content: </> Bootstrapper.bindToPreAnimation( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.PreAnimation.

searchDocs.title: bindToPreRender​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bootstrapper.bindToPreRender( modules: LoadedModules | ModuleScripts,-- The array of loaded modules or ModuleScripts methodName: string-- The method to execute. Prefix with '.' for static calls or ':' for object methods. ) → Cleanup-- A cleanup function to disconnect the event. Hooks a specified method in all modules to RunService.PreRender.

searchDocs.title: Why Bootstrapper?​
searchDocs.content: As Roblox projects scale, developers often fall into the trap of decentralized execution. Every module, controller or service connects to RunService independently. Before long, you have dozens of scripts updating independently every frame. Without strict control over whether your InputSystem runs before or after your CharacterController, you inevitably run into state bugs, race conditions, and one-frame delays.  Bootstrapper solves this by treating execution flow as a single, managed pipeline. By organizing your modules into simple arrays and updating them in a strict sequence, you completely eliminate unpredictable load orders and execution desyncs. Bootstrapper makes sure things happen exactly when you tell them to, and doesn't care if you write OOP, functional, or procedural code.

searchDocs.title: Features​
searchDocs.content: Deterministic Execution: Load modules alphabetically or provide a strict manual sequence. Your game will start exactly the same way every single time.Centralized Scheduler: Iterate over your modules and fire their updates synchronously.Luau Method Syntax: Use standard dot or colon notation (. or :) in your string arguments to explicitly call static functions or inject self for object methods.Flexible Event Binding: Hook a sequence of modules directly to RunService events, RBXScriptSignals, or custom Signal objects.Automatic Memory Profiling: Automatically assigns debug.setmemorycategory to every module's thread and RunService connection so your MicroProfiler is readable.Lazy Require: Pass raw ModuleScript instances directly as argument. Bootstrapper will automatically require and cache them for you.

searchDocs.title: Wally​
searchDocs.content: Add this to your wally.toml:  ldgerrits/bootstrapper@^1.0.16

searchDocs.title: Quick Start​
searchDocs.content: local Bootstrapper = require(packages.Bootstrapper) -- Strict boot sequence (manual order for core systems) local bootSequence = { path.to.DataService, path.to.PlayerService, path.to.ZoneService } Bootstrapper.run(bootSequence, ':init') -- injects self Bootstrapper.runAsync(bootSequence, ':start') -- injects self -- Automatic discovery (A-Z Sorting) local systems = Bootstrapper.loadChildren(path.to.Systems, Bootstrapper.byName('System$')) -- Execution choice -- Use 'run' for a strict A-Z sequence, or 'runParallel' if not Bootstrapper.run(systems, '.init') -- does NOT inject self Bootstrapper.runParallel(systems, '.start') -- does NOT inject self -- Deterministic runtime (only binds modules with 'onUpdate') -- Maintains alphabetical execution every frame with auto-memory profiling. Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat(systems, '.onUpdate') -- does NOT inject self

searchDocs.title: 1. Finding & Loading Modules​
searchDocs.content: Load modules from a folder. Bootstrapper automatically requires them and returns a deterministically sorted array (alphabetical by Name), ensuring your game starts the same way every time.  local Bootstrapper = require(path.to.Bootstrapper) local services, errors = Bootstrapper.loadDescendants(path.to.Services, Bootstrapper.byName('Service$'))

searchDocs.title: 2. Manual Sequences​
searchDocs.content: You can use loadSequence to define a manual order using an array ModuleScript instances. This sequence can be stored and reused for all lifecycle stages.  local services, errors = Bootstrapper.loadSequence({ path.to.DataService, path.to.PlayerService, path.to.AssetService, }) -- Use the resulting services array for everything else Bootstrapper.run(services, '.init')

searchDocs.title: 3. Dot vs. Colon Notation​
searchDocs.content: When passing a method name to any Bootstrapper function, you can dictate how the function is executed:  'methodName' or ':methodName': Calls as an object method. module:methodName() '.methodName' (Static): Calls as a static function. module.methodName()  -- Injects 'self' into every module's 'onUpdate' method Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat(services, ':onUpdate') -- Calls 'tick' statically, without passing the module table Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat(services, '.tick')

searchDocs.title: 4. Lazy Resolution​
searchDocs.content: You don't have to call a 'load' function. You can also just pass an array of ModuleScripts directly to events, and the Bootstrapper will handle the require() and memory profiling for you automatically.  -- This works even if the modules haven't been required yet Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat({ path.to.CombatService, path.to.VFXService, }, '.onUpdate')

searchDocs.title: 5. Execution Flow​
searchDocs.content: Trigger methods across your modules. run yields the current thread until all modules finish, while parallel fires them in background threads.  -- Sequential & Blocking -- Yields until every module's '.init' finishes in order. -- Returns an array of successful modules and a map of errors. local loaded, errors = Bootstrapper.run(services, 'init') -- Sequential & Non-blocking -- Executes the sequence in the background without yielding the caller. -- Maintains your deterministic order of operations. Bootstrapper.runAsync(services, 'postInit') -- Parallel & Non-blocking -- Fires all methods simultaneously for maximum speed. -- Best for independent tasks where execution order doesn't matter. Bootstrapper.runParallel(services, 'start', gameData)

searchDocs.title: 6. Event Binding​
searchDocs.content: Route RunService events or signals directly to module methods. All binding functions follow the same signature: (modules, methodName, [context]).  local cleanupHeartbeat = Bootstrapper.bindToHeartbeat(services, 'onHeartbeat') local cleanupRenderStep = Bootstrapper.bindToRenderStep(services, 'onRender', Enum.RenderPriority.Last.Value) local cleanupGameState = Bootstrapper.bindTo(services, 'onGameState', GameState.Changed) -- Disconnect everything when they are no longer needed cleanupHeartbeat() cleanupRenderStep() cleanupGameState()
